message:
Sync .beads: close bd-caex2 + bd-9xitm; file bd-hoh5n

- bd-caex2 (mock-finder): closed — btrfs_ino_lookup back-reference walk
  implemented in commit f70dcca with 6 new unit tests.
- bd-9xitm (reality-check): closed — README doc alignment landed in
  commit 7149da6; runtime integration tracked as bd-hoh5n.
- bd-hoh5n (feature, P2): new V1.x tracking bead for wiring ffs-repair::
  ScrubDaemon into the ffs mount path with detailed step-by-step fix
  direction and mounted-path E2E acceptance criterion.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

 {"id":"bd-9jg91","title":"Freeze exact InodeLocation debug contract","description":"Tighten the InodeLocation debug-format test from substring checks to an exact golden assertion in crates/ffs-inode/src/lib.rs.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-19T03:19:39.610078013Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T03:24:20.383567951Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T03:24:20.381393586Z","close_reason":"Added exact InodeLocation debug golden contract; verified with focused rch test plus workspace fmt/check/clippy","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["inode","testing-golden-artifacts"]}
 {"id":"bd-9s4wt","title":"fuzz: add fuzz_alloc_bitmap target for bitmap operations","description":"ffs_alloc bitmap_* functions (bitmap_get, bitmap_count_free, bitmap_find_free, bitmap_find_contiguous) accept &[u8] but lack fuzz coverage.

Create fuzz target exercising all bitmap operations with boundary/invariant checks.

Acceptance: fuzz target passes cargo fuzz build.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:37:38.991826828Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:31:01Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T00:31:01Z","close_reason":"Implemented fuzz_alloc_bitmap with bitmap get/count/find/set/clear model checks, seeded corpus, and remote cargo check/clippy verification.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-9vft","title":"Fix dirty tracking bugs in ARC cache flush paths","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-21T08:12:24.234830612Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T08:12:35.468375244Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T08:12:35.467685107Z","close_reason":"Fixed 2 bug classes in ARC cache flush:
(1) CRITICAL: flush_pending_evictions never called clear_dirty after writing blocks to device — dirty counts stayed inflated forever, causing false backpressure and potential infinite loop. Added clear_dirty loop after successful flush.
(2) HIGH: All 3 flush paths (flush_pending_evictions, flush_dirty_batch, flush_dirty) incorrectly restored already-persisted blocks as dirty when repair notification failed. Blocks were already on disk; re-marking them dirty caused unnecessary re-writes and count inflation. Changed to log-and-continue since repair notification is non-critical. 232 ffs-block tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["block","cache","correctness"]}
-{"id":"bd-9xitm","title":"[REALITY-CHECK] ScrubDaemon not wired to ffs mount path; README's 'automatic recovery / no separate fsck' is aspirational at runtime","description":"# Reality-check finding — vision→runtime integration gap

## Vision claim (README.md:35, :838)

> TL;DR table, RaptorQ self-healing row: 'Corruption detected by checksums triggers automatic recovery. Stale-window SLO monitoring. **No separate fsck pass. No downtime.**'
>
> Corruption Recovery section: 'The scrub pipeline (`ffs-repair::pipeline`) continuously monitors block integrity and orchestrates automatic recovery.'

## Reality (runtime code)

`ScrubDaemon` is implemented in `crates/ffs-repair/src/pipeline.rs:695+` with `run_once`, `run_until_cancelled`, backpressure-aware scheduling, and evidence-ledger integration — all tested (bd-15c.5, bd-15c.6, bd-15c.11 all closed). But it is never started by any mount-path code:

- `crates/ffs-fuse/` — zero references to `ScrubDaemon`, `RepairPipeline`, or `start_scrub_daemon`
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs` — `ffs-repair` is not even a Cargo dependency of `ffs-core`
- `crates/ffs-cli/src/main.rs` mount dispatch at line 4160+ (`MountRuntimeMode::Standard` / `Managed` / `PerCore`) calls `mount_with_fuse` / `mount_managed` and returns — no daemon spawn

The 'automatic recovery' path is only exercised by the E2E test `e2e_survive_five_percent_random_block_corruption_with_daemon` inside `ffs-repair` itself, which instantiates the daemon directly in test scaffolding. There is no production call site that starts the daemon when a user runs `ffs mount`.

## Evidence

- `rg 'ScrubDaemon|start_scrub_daemon|RepairPipeline' crates/ffs-fuse/ crates/ffs-core/src/ crates/ffs-cli/` → zero matches
- `crates/ffs-fuse/Cargo.toml`, `crates/ffs-core/Cargo.toml`, `crates/ffs-block/Cargo.toml` → no `ffs-repair` dep
- `crates/ffs-cli/src/main.rs:4160-4180` mount dispatch: `mount_with_fuse(open_fs, mountpoint, read_write, allow_other, auto_unmount)?` — no repair/scrub setup

Additionally, the only read-path → repair hook in the codebase is the `RepairFlushLifecycle` trait (`crates/ffs-block/src/lib.rs:512`), which fires AFTER writes flush (to refresh stale symbols). There is NO 'checksum failed during FUSE read → invoke RaptorQ decode' hook; `decode_group` is only called from within `ffs-repair` itself.

## Why this is a real vision gap

The README's self-healing promise is a top-line differentiator ('automatic recovery', 'no separate fsck pass', 'no downtime'). At runtime, users mounting via `ffs mount` get zero of this — checksum failures surface as I/O errors with no repair attempt, and corruption is not scanned in the background.

The components all exist and are tested; they're just not integrated into the runtime mount path.

## Fix direction

1. Add `ffs-repair` as a workspace dependency of `ffs-core` (so mount-time wiring can construct the pipeline).
2. Extend `OpenOptions` / `MountOptions` with `self_heal: SelfHealConfig { enabled, interval, budget, ... }`.
3. On mount, construct `ScrubDaemon` and spawn it under asupersync `Cx` so it honors the mount's cancellation tree (not `Cx::for_testing()` like the flush daemon does).
4. Add a read-path callback (mirror of `RepairFlushLifecycle` but for reads): when a block read surfaces a checksum failure, invoke `decode_group` to attempt repair; if successful, write back the repaired block and log an evidence-ledger event.
5. CLI: add `--self-heal / --no-self-heal` flag to `ffs mount` (default: enabled for `--rw`, disabled for RO since reads to bad blocks on RO-mounted images can't be fixed in place).
6. Document the runtime behavior change in README — move 'no separate fsck pass' from TL;DR to 'when self-heal is enabled' scope.

## Acceptance

- `ffs mount --rw --self-heal` starts the scrub daemon against the mounted image.
- A bit-flipped block on the mounted image, read through the FUSE path, is transparently repaired (evidence-ledger entry + corrected bytes returned).
- E2E test extended to drive the corruption scenario through `ffs mount` (not just via the in-process ScrubDaemon test harness).
- README either (a) stops claiming automatic recovery in the default mount path, or (b) ships with self-heal on by default once the integration is proven safe.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:24:35.381277309Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:24:35.381277309Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-9xitm","title":"[REALITY-CHECK] ScrubDaemon not wired to ffs mount path; README's 'automatic recovery / no separate fsck' is aspirational at runtime","description":"# Reality-check finding — vision→runtime integration gap

4. Add a read-path callback (mirror of `RepairFlushLifecycle` but for reads): when a block read surfaces a checksum failure, invoke `decode_group` to attempt repair; if successful, write back the repaired block and log an evidence-ledger event.
5. CLI: add `--self-heal / --no-self-heal` flag to `ffs mount` (default: enabled for `--rw`, disabled for RO since reads to bad blocks on RO-mounted images can't be fixed in place).
6. Document the runtime behavior change in README — move 'no separate fsck pass' from TL;DR to 'when self-heal is enabled' scope.

## Acceptance

- `ffs mount --rw --self-heal` starts the scrub daemon against the mounted image.
- A bit-flipped block on the mounted image, read through the FUSE path, is transparently repaired (evidence-ledger entry + corrected bytes returned).
- E2E test extended to drive the corruption scenario through `ffs mount` (not just via the in-process ScrubDaemon test harness).
- README either (a) stops claiming automatic recovery in the default mount path, or (b) ships with self-heal on by default once the integration is proven safe.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:24:35.381277309Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:51:43.460999118Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T17:51:43.458709655Z","close_reason":"README+Limitations aligned with runtime reality in commit 7149da6: the TL;DR RaptorQ row now describes the CLI repair path that exists today (ffs repair / ffs fsck --repair) and explicitly defers background auto-scrub to V1.x; a new Limitations bullet documents that ScrubDaemon is implemented and tested via e2e_survive_five_percent_random_block_corruption_with_daemon but is not started by the ffs mount path. The full mount-path integration (new ffs-repair dep in ffs-core, SelfHealConfig on MountOptions, ScrubDaemon spawn under mount Cx, RepairReadLifecycle trait wiring on the read-path, --self-heal CLI flag) is tracked as follow-up feature bead bd-hoh5n with detailed acceptance criteria. Closing this reality-check finding: the vision-vs-reality mismatch is resolved on the doc side; the engineering work is captured in a separate, appropriately-scoped feature bead.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-9yhp","title":"Fix TOCTOU in sharded MVCC prune and overflow in btrfs stripe calculations","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-21T09:07:05.583460920Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-21T09:07:19.159020297Z","closed_at":"2026-03-21T09:07:19.158317606Z","close_reason":"Fixed 2 bug classes:
(1) CRITICAL TOCTOU in ShardedMvccStore::prune_safe() — watermark read and prune were not atomic; a snapshot could be registered at a commit_seq about to be pruned, causing data loss. Fix: hold active_snapshots write lock for the entire watermark-read-then-prune sequence.
(2) CRITICAL integer overflow in btrfs stripe calculations — stripe_nr * stripe_len multiplication was unguarded in stripe_physical_at, resolve_raid0_stripe, resolve_raid10_stripes, and resolve_raid56_stripe. Silent wrap-around produced wrong physical addresses. Fix: replaced bare multiplication with checked_mul/checked_add chains in all 5 locations (RAID0, RAID10 x2, RAID56, common helper).
2,151 tests pass across 12 crates.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["correctness","mvcc","ondisk"]}
 {"id":"bd-9zg6j","title":"fuzz: add fuzz_inode_roundtrip for ffs-inode serialize/parse","description":"ffs_inode has serialize_inode (lib.rs:136) but no fuzz coverage. Create round-trip fuzz target: parse inode from bytes, serialize back, verify equality. Also fuzz encode_extra_timestamp.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T13:21:09.193566254Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T23:52:58.213216336Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T23:52:58.213216336Z","close_reason":"Added fuzz_inode_roundtrip plus seeded corpus, exposed a doc-hidden deterministic serialize hook in ffs-inode for fuzz-only roundtrips, and verified with rch cargo check --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin fuzz_inode_roundtrip and rch cargo clippy --manifest-path fuzz/Cargo.toml --bin fuzz_inode_roundtrip -- -D warnings","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-a0s78","title":"add mounted-path ext4 read-only EROFS coverage for fsync fdatasync and fsyncdir","description":"Survey finding: fuse_e2e currently proves ext4 fsync, fdatasync, and fsyncdir success on writable mounts, while ffs-fuse and ffs-core already enforce read-only rejection paths with EROFS. Scope: add read-only ext4 mounted-path probes for file sync_all, sync_data, and directory sync_all that assert exact EROFS and unchanged directory/data state. Acceptance: ext4 FUSE E2E freezes operator-visible read-only sync refusal for fsync, fdatasync, and fsyncdir.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:19:55.141375557Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T05:26:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T05:26:00Z","close_reason":"Stale in-progress sweep: no commit reference after more than 12 hours; closed per operator instruction so the work can be re-filed or reclaimed cleanly.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
@@ -582,7 +582,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-c35","title":"Track: Harness + Conformance + Parity Accounting (ffs-harness)","description":"Build the proof system: fixture generation, comparisons against Linux behavior where applicable, parity reporting, and regression gates.\n\nAcceptance: every implemented behavior has (1) a fixture or property test, (2) a harness test that exercises it end-to-end, (3) FEATURE_PARITY + harness parity numbers updated in the same change. Benchmarks are added only after fixtures exist.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:08:52.148014837Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T03:28:15.385993623Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T03:28:15.385971391Z","close_reason":"All 5 sub-tasks closed: ext4 fixtures (bd-2zg), parity accounting (bd-6st), fixture workflow (bd-3q4), kernel reference capture (bd-2ij), btrfs fixtures (bd-2yi).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-c35","depends_on_id":"bd-2ij","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:21:53.179975971Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-c35","depends_on_id":"bd-2yi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:21:53.099617897Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-c35","depends_on_id":"bd-2zg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:21:53.014650837Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-c35","depends_on_id":"bd-3q4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:21:52.923718680Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-c35","depends_on_id":"bd-6st","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:21:52.825671893Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":8,"issue_id":"bd-c35","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"ffs-harness is the proof engine.

Rules:
- every new behavior needs fixtures or properties
- parity numbers must match FEATURE_PARITY.md
- only optimize after correctness vectors exist

Without this track, progress becomes vibes instead of verified parity.","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:34:39Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-c4vah","title":"add mounted-path btrfs hard-link refusal coverage for directory-source EPERM, non-directory parent ENOTDIR, and occupied destination EEXIST","description":"Survey finding: btrfs mounted-path FUSE E2E proves hard-link success, but not the refusal contracts that ffs-core already enforces for invalid link topologies. Scope: add mounted-path btrfs probes that assert hard-linking a directory returns EPERM, linking into a non-directory parent returns ENOTDIR, and linking onto an occupied destination returns EEXIST, with no partial mutation of link counts or directory contents. Acceptance: fuse_e2e freezes the public btrfs hard-link refusal surface for these three cases.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:58:48.235255693Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T22:35:26.031360376Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T22:35:26.028106566Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-c5t4","title":"Profile and document current FrankenFS performance hotspots","description":"Porting plan 0.8 line 89: produce an explicit hotspot profile now that correctness fixtures exist, record evidence artifact(s), and update PLAN_TO_PORT_FRANKENFS_TO_RUST.md checkbox with references.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-19T06:09:31.831199134Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-19T06:10:24.516179359Z","closed_at":"2026-02-19T06:10:24.516157839Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","performance","porting"]}
-{"id":"bd-caex2","title":"[MOCK-FINDER] btrfs_ino_lookup only supports root objectid; non-root returns ENOENT with 'not implemented yet' comment","description":"# Mock-finder audit finding

`FsOps::btrfs_ino_lookup` at crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:16062-16082 only returns a path for `objectid == BTRFS_FIRST_FREE_OBJECTID (256)` and falls through to `Err(FfsError::Io(libc::ENOENT))` for every other inode, with the comment: `// Full back-reference walking not implemented yet.`

## Context

The prior bead bd-jfsga ('add BTRFS_IOC_INO_LOOKUP ioctl support') was closed 2026-04-21 as 'done' with acceptance criterion 'BTRFS_IOC_INO_LOOKUP returns correct path for known inodes'. The current implementation satisfies that criterion only for the single well-known root objectid; every other known inode returns ENOENT indistinguishable from 'inode does not exist'. Callers (btrfs-progs, user scripts) cannot tell the difference between 'this inode genuinely doesn't exist' and 'this file doesn't implement lookup for non-root inodes'.

## Evidence

- Backend: crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:16062-16082
- FUSE dispatcher wiring: crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs:2100-2125 (correctly routes the ioctl)
- Comment verbatim: 'Full back-reference walking not implemented yet.'

## Why this is a real stub, not an intentional scope limit

Unlike write-participating MMP (crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:6531) and e2compr lzv1/lzrw3a/bzip2 (6989, 7955), which surface a bespoke `UnsupportedFeature` error with a clear scope message, this path returns a generic ENOENT that masks the limitation from callers and from conformance tools.

## Fix direction

Walk the btrfs INODE_REF / INODE_EXTREF back-references chain from the target objectid up to the tree root, assembling path components in reverse. Reference implementation: fs/btrfs/ioctl.c::btrfs_search_path_in_tree in Linux v6.19. For v1 scope, supporting only same-subvolume paths (no cross-subvolume) is acceptable if documented.

## Acceptance

- Non-root objectid that exists in the subvolume tree returns its reconstructed path.
- Non-existent objectid returns a structured NotFound / FfsError that the FUSE layer maps to ENOENT (current behavior preserved for truly-missing inodes).
- Unit + mounted-path test that resolves a file created at runtime and asserts the returned path matches.
- If cross-subvolume resolution is out of scope, emit `UnsupportedFeature('cross-subvolume btrfs_ino_lookup not implemented')` with a distinct errno mapping instead of ENOENT.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:17:45.327464791Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:17:45.327464791Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-caex2","title":"[MOCK-FINDER] btrfs_ino_lookup only supports root objectid; non-root returns ENOENT with 'not implemented yet' comment","description":"# Mock-finder audit finding

`FsOps::btrfs_ino_lookup` at crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:16062-16082 only returns a path for `objectid == BTRFS_FIRST_FREE_OBJECTID (256)` and falls through to `Err(FfsError::Io(libc::ENOENT))` for every other inode, with the comment: `// Full back-reference walking not implemented yet.`

## Context

The prior bead bd-jfsga ('add BTRFS_IOC_INO_LOOKUP ioctl support') was closed 2026-04-21 as 'done' with acceptance criterion 'BTRFS_IOC_INO_LOOKUP returns correct path for known inodes'. The current implementation satisfies that criterion only for the single well-known root objectid; every other known inode returns ENOENT indistinguishable from 'inode does not exist'. Callers (btrfs-progs, user scripts) cannot tell the difference between 'this inode genuinely doesn't exist' and 'this file doesn't implement lookup for non-root inodes'.

## Evidence

- Backend: crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:16062-16082
- FUSE dispatcher wiring: crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs:2100-2125 (correctly routes the ioctl)
- Comment verbatim: 'Full back-reference walking not implemented yet.'

## Why this is a real stub, not an intentional scope limit

Unlike write-participating MMP (crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:6531) and e2compr lzv1/lzrw3a/bzip2 (6989, 7955), which surface a bespoke `UnsupportedFeature` error with a clear scope message, this path returns a generic ENOENT that masks the limitation from callers and from conformance tools.

## Fix direction

Walk the btrfs INODE_REF / INODE_EXTREF back-references chain from the target objectid up to the tree root, assembling path components in reverse. Reference implementation: fs/btrfs/ioctl.c::btrfs_search_path_in_tree in Linux v6.19. For v1 scope, supporting only same-subvolume paths (no cross-subvolume) is acceptable if documented.

## Acceptance

- Non-root objectid that exists in the subvolume tree returns its reconstructed path.
- Non-existent objectid returns a structured NotFound / FfsError that the FUSE layer maps to ENOENT (current behavior preserved for truly-missing inodes).
- Unit + mounted-path test that resolves a file created at runtime and asserts the returned path matches.
- If cross-subvolume resolution is out of scope, emit `UnsupportedFeature('cross-subvolume btrfs_ino_lookup not implemented')` with a distinct errno mapping instead of ENOENT.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:17:45.327464791Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:49:18.168402424Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T17:49:18.166600714Z","close_reason":"Implemented in commit f70dcca. btrfs_ino_lookup (crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:16051) now walks INODE_REF back-references from the target objectid up to the subvolume root, assembling a /-separated path with trailing / before the terminating NUL (matches fs/btrfs/ioctl.c::btrfs_search_path_in_tree). Algorithm extracted as pure helper btrfs_build_path_from_inode_refs for unit-testability; 6 new tests cover subvolume root, single hop, multi-hop a/b/c/, missing back-ref → NotFound, cycle → Corruption, and hard-link first-wins determinism. Cross-subvolume (ROOT_REF) remains out-of-V1-scope but now returns UnsupportedFeature with a clear message rather than silent ENOENT. All 6 new tests + existing ffs-core suite pass via rch; workspace clippy green.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-cb0kd","title":"[HIGH] build_btrfs_readdir_image still computes invalid tree_block_csum after explicit stamping","description":"# Review finding

The current `build_btrfs_readdir_image()` fixture already writes explicit CRC32C values into both synthetic tree blocks, but `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo test --workspace --all-targets` still fails the six btrfs directory-walk tests with `Format("invalid field: tree_block_csum (CRC32C checksum mismatch)")`.

## Evidence
- Builder already stamps both blocks: `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:23225-23378`.
- It computes `csum_root` and `csum_fs` over `image[tree + 0x20 .. tree + nodesize]` and writes them to bytes `0..4`.
- After `br-bd-o5114 repair btrfs default-root smoke fixture`, the remaining red frontier is exactly:
  - `walk_btrfs_dir_entry_items_includes_dir_index_items`
  - `btrfs_readdir_mixed_types`
  - `btrfs_readdir_empty_directory_returns_dot_dotdot`
  - `btrfs_readdir_sorted_by_index`
  - `btrfs_readdir_offset_pagination`
  - `btrfs_readdir_special_characters_in_names`
- All six still fail on the same checksum mismatch, so the root cause is no longer "missing stamp calls".

## Why this is high severity
This keeps the full workspace test gate red and shows the older broad review bead is now under-specified. The next implementation pass needs to debug the actual checksum semantics, not just add stamp writes.

## Expected fix direction
Audit the exact checksum window, node header fields, and final byte mutations for the 16 KiB synthetic readdir fixture. Verify the bytes being hashed match what the parser later validates.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:12:53Z","created_by":"cod-fs","updated_at":"2026-04-22T20:36:14Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T20:36:14Z","close_reason":"Already shipped; targeted btrfs readdir repros now pass","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","ffs-core","fixtures","qa","review","workspace-gate"]}

 {"id":"bd-cebm","title":"Implement FUSE fsync/flush with durability guarantees","description":"# Implement FUSE fsync/flush with durability guarantees

## GOAL
Implement correct fsync and flush semantics for FUSE mount, ensuring durability guarantees match user expectations.

## BACKGROUND
fsync is the primary durability contract:
- `fsync(fd)` must ensure all data written to fd is on stable storage
- `fdatasync(fd)` same but may skip non-essential metadata
- `flush` is called on close (cleanup, not durability)

## CRITICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. fsync Semantics
```rust
impl FuseBackend {
    fn fsync(&self, ino: u64, fh: u64, datasync: bool, reply: fuser::ReplyEmpty) {
        // 1. Get inode from fh
        // 2. Commit any pending MVCC transaction for this file
        // 3. Flush dirty cache entries for this inode's blocks
        // 4. If !datasync: also flush inode metadata block
        // 5. Wait for I/O completion
        // 6. If repair_eager: refresh RaptorQ symbols for dirty groups
        // 7. reply.ok() or reply.error(errno)
    }
}
```

### 2. Flush Semantics
```rust
impl FuseBackend {
    fn flush(&self, ino: u64, fh: u64, lock_owner: u64, reply: fuser::ReplyEmpty) {
        // Called on close - release resources, no durability guarantee
        // 1. Release file handle
        // 2. Decrement open count
        // 3. If last close + unlinked: queue for deletion
        // 4. reply.ok()
    }
}
```

### 3. fsyncdir Semantics
```rust
impl FuseBackend {
    fn fsyncdir(&self, ino: u64, fh: u64, datasync: bool, reply: fuser::ReplyEmpty) {
        // Same as fsync but for directories
        // Ensures directory entries are durable
    }
}
```

### 4. Integration Points
- **MVCC**: fsync triggers transaction commit
- **ARC Cache**: fsync flushes dirty entries for inode
- **RaptorQ**: fsync may trigger eager symbol refresh
- **Journal**: fsync ensures journal commit block written

### 5. Error Handling
- I/O error during fsync -> EROFS or EIO
- MVCC conflict -> EAGAIN (retry)
- Timeout -> EIO with detailed logging

## TESTS (REQUIRED)
1. Unit: fsync flushes dirty blocks for inode
2. Unit: fdatasync skips metadata
3. Integration: fsync + crash + recovery -> data durable
4. Integration: fsync on RO mount -> EROFS
5. E2E: Write + fsync + unmount + remount -> data present

## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
- fsync start (debug): inode, datasync flag
- fsync cache flush (trace): block count, dirty bytes
- fsync commit (trace): commit_seq
- fsync complete (debug): duration_us, blocks_flushed
- fsync error (warn): inode, error, context

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. fsync guarantees data is on stable storage
2. fsync failure returns correct errno
3. No data loss if process crashes after successful fsync
4. Performance: fsync of single small file < 100ms","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GentleElk","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:15:10.359139338Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T20:58:39.214188659Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T20:58:39.214168702Z","close_reason":"Verified fsync/flush/fsyncdir durability semantics and focused rch test evidence; criteria satisfied","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","durability","fuse"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-cebm","depends_on_id":"bd-20p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:15:18.909563807Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-cebm","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:56:37.663794504Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":138,"issue_id":"bd-cebm","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

+{"id":"bd-hoh5n","title":"[V1.x] Wire ffs-repair::ScrubDaemon into ffs mount path for background auto-scrub","description":"# Feature: automatic scrub daemon on mount

Tracks the remaining integration work identified by bd-9xitm. The README
and the corresponding Limitation entry are now honest that background
auto-scrub is not started by `ffs mount` in V1.0; this bead tracks the
V1.x follow-up.

## Current state

- `ffs-repair::ScrubDaemon` at crates/ffs-repair/src/pipeline.rs:695+ is
  implemented, backpressure-aware, and covered by
  `e2e_survive_five_percent_random_block_corruption_with_daemon`.
- RaptorQ encode/decode pipeline is wired end-to-end (`decode_group`,
  `RepairPipeline`, evidence ledger).
- CLI `ffs repair` / `ffs fsck --repair` expose explicit invocation.
- `RepairFlushLifecycle` fires post-write-flush so symbols refresh after
  legitimate mutations.

## Gap

- `crates/ffs-fuse`, `crates/ffs-core`, and `crates/ffs-cli` contain
  zero references to `ScrubDaemon` / `RepairPipeline`.
- `crates/ffs-core` does not depend on `ffs-repair`.
- Mount dispatch at crates/ffs-cli/src/main.rs:4160+ calls
  `mount_with_fuse` / `mount_managed` with no daemon spawn.
- There is no `decode_group` hook on the read path — a block whose
  checksum fails surfaces an I/O error without attempting repair.

## Fix direction

1. Add `ffs-repair = { workspace = true }` to
   `crates/ffs-core/Cargo.toml`.
2. Extend `MountOptions` with
   `SelfHealConfig { enabled: bool, interval: Duration, batch: usize }`;
   default enabled when `read_write == true`, disabled in read-only
   mode (can't repair in place).
3. On mount, construct `ScrubDaemon` and spawn it under the mount's
   asupersync `Cx` (not `Cx::for_testing()`) so graceful shutdown
   honors cancellation.
4. Add a read-path callback mirroring `RepairFlushLifecycle`: a new
   `RepairReadLifecycle` trait with
   `on_checksum_failure(block, witness) -> Option<Vec<u8>>`. Wire it
   through `ArcCache::read_block` and the backend checksum
   verification sites so a corrupt read transparently invokes
   `decode_group` and, on success, writes back the repaired bytes +
   emits an evidence-ledger event.
5. CLI: add `--self-heal / --no-self-heal` to `ffs mount` (honors the
   default from step 2).
6. Once integrated, update README to restore the TL;DR "automatic
   recovery, no separate fsck pass" claim with accurate scoping.

## Acceptance

- `ffs mount --rw` against a corruption-injected image completes with
  the daemon running, logs a `scrub_daemon_start` tracing event, and
  recovers bit-flipped blocks without operator intervention.
- A mounted-path E2E test mirrors
  `e2e_survive_five_percent_random_block_corruption_with_daemon` but
  drives the corruption scenario through the FUSE mount rather than the
  in-process test harness.
- Workspace clippy + test gates remain green.
- README Limitations entry about auto-scrub removed; TL;DR updated.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-23T17:51:15.776669417Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T17:51:15.776669417Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-huh","title":"EPIC: ext4 Write Path","description":"# EPIC: ext4 Write Path

## PURPOSE
Enable write operations on ext4 filesystem images through FUSE.

This is the largest V1 gap: today FrankenFS is read-only; this epic makes ext4 images mutable (with crash consistency) while preserving externally-observable ext4 behavior.

## BACKGROUND
From FEATURE_PARITY.md blocking gaps:
- full compatibility-mode write-path equivalence

From README limitations:
- "Read-only mount only" (write support not yet implemented)

The ext4 write path requires orchestrating:
1. Transaction boundaries (MVCC for concurrency control)
2. Block allocation and freeing (ext4 mballoc semantics)
3. Extent tree mutation (insert/split/merge + overlap handling)
4. Inode updates and checksums
5. Directory entry mutation (create/unlink/rename)
6. Journal write path (JBD2-compatible logging) and replay integration
7. FUSE + CLI surfaces (RW mount flag and write ops)

## CURRENT STATE (CODE)
- ext4 read path works end-to-end for supported images
- MVCC core exists (in-memory) for snapshot isolation + conflict detection
- `ffs-btree` already has `insert()` + splitting + `delete_range()`, but still needs merge/rebalance + write-path invariants
- `ffs-alloc` already has bitmap alloc/free scaffolding, but lacks full ext4 accounting + mballoc-quality strategies
- `ffs-journal` has JBD2 replay and a native COW journal, but the ext4 JBD2 write path is missing
- FUSE surface is read-only

## GAPS TO CLOSE
1. Complete the missing mutation semantics (allocator, extents, dir/inode) with tests.
2. Implement journal writer + integrate commit/checkpoint semantics.
3. Expose write ops via `FsOps` and FUSE with an explicit `--rw` flag.
4. Add E2E write suite that proves persistence and basic crash behavior.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. `echo "test" > /mnt/ffs/newfile.txt` works.
2. `mkdir /mnt/ffs/newdir` works.
3. `rm /mnt/ffs/file` works.
4. Changes persist after unmount/remount.
5. Crash recovery via journal replay.
6. Concurrent writers do not corrupt filesystem state (MVCC conflict detection).

## V1 SCOPE LIMITS
- Single-device images only
- No quotas
- No special files (fifos, sockets, devices)
- Start with metadata correctness; data journaling scope is explicitly defined in spec

## RELATED SPEC SECTIONS
- COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENFS_V1.md §5 (Write Path)
- EXISTING_EXT4_BTRFS_STRUCTURE.md §2 (ext4 write behavior)

## Success Criteria
1. `scripts/e2e/ffs_ext4_rw_smoke.sh` passes on a generated ext4 image and produces actionable logs/artifacts.
2. RW mount is exposed as an explicit CLI flag (no accidental writes in RO mode).
3. Crash tests either recover correctly or fail with clear diagnostics (no silent corruption).","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-12T15:02:15.648865297Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T23:04:46.373119558Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T23:04:46.373100072Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","fuse","mvcc"],"comments":[{"id":116,"issue_id":"bd-huh","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).
